tiles.interactText: BERTH ROW B — Long-term moorings. Most ships here haven't moved in years.
Private Toilet
Impound Clerk's counter. A half-eaten lunch and a ledger full of fines.
A desk drawer left unlocked. Rudo's gambling notes and a spare key tag.
Private Bathroom Door
Cradle 1 — blast-scorched landing strut. Something heavy came in fast.
Cradle 3 — tagged 'CIVIC RESERVE'. An old designation nobody respects anymore.
Checkpoint gate to the Civic Impound. A bored enforcer leans on the frame.

tiles.lockHint: Requires the impound key from Commander Vask.
tiles.interactText: Heavy civic security door. Requires the impound key or a convincing story.
A rusted berth lockbox with a broken seal.

BERTH E-7 — CIVIC IMPOUND — 'SCAVENGER'S WAKE' — Impounded Cycle 1847. Reason: Outstanding race violations, unlicensed modifications, unpaid port fees. Vessel flagged for criminal investigation. DO NOT ACCESS WITHOUT CLEARANCE.
The impound bay door. You'll need the clerk's key, a launch window, and someone who can fly this thing.

transition.text: The heavy bay door grinds open. The Wake smells like sealed metal and old ambition.

tiles.interactText: A scav's kit left in a hurry. Still has some use.
A maintenance grate hangs open. The vents below smell like old fuel and bad decisions.

transition.text: You drop into the station's lower vents.

tiles.interactText: Officer's Table Only
Trader Moss's General Stall — rations, basic gear, overpriced water cells. She eyes you like she knows something.
Fence Davi's 'Import' Stall — no questions asked. Prices depend on what you've done today.
Naia's desk. A surface covered in favours owed and debts collected. She sits behind it like a judge.
Patch-Lan's diagnostic station. The scanner is old but still works. It would probably notice something unusual about your biology.
A bulletin board covered in missing-person notices, debt warnings, and one water-damaged wanted poster. The face on it is familiar.
A side passage runs toward the Civic Quarter. A camera watches the entrance.
Emergency medical stores. Patch-Lan keeps them stocked out of stubbornness.
A market crate someone forgot to lock.
A barrel knocked over in the last scuffle. Salvage inside.
A personal strongbox under Naia's table. She definitely knows it's been opened.
A shelf of unlabelled medical texts and handwritten notes. One file is labeled 'CLONE RECOVERY PROTOCOLS — THEORETICAL'.
Authority checkpoint. A scanner arch and a bored guard who looks like he'd prefer literally anything else to happen.
The launch scheduling terminal. Departure windows for every berth, updated in real-time. You'd need the right access code — or someone who has one.
Commander Vask's command terminal. Access logs, impound records, and what looks like a personal financial file.
A grimy junction where the civic drains meet the underbelly access ducts. Someone has scratched directions into the wall — they're not civic-approved.
A worn card table in the back room above Torsh's bar. The felt is patched, the lighting is bad, and the players are better than they look.
Clerk Mori's workstation. A mug of cold tea and a dossier labeled 'PERSONAL — DO NOT FILE'.
A locked locker in the authority office. Inside: the impound release key for Berth E-7.
Station records going back decades. Most of it is boring. One file is about a ship called the Scavenger's Wake.
A heavy cell-block door. The lock is civic-standard and can be opened from this side.

transition.text: You push through into the cell block.

tiles.interactText: Torsh's bar. A counter made of salvaged hull plating. The drinks look worse than they taste, which is impressive.
A wall covered in old race results. One result is circled repeatedly in red grease-pen: 'WAKE — DISQUALIFIED'. No year given.
A maintenance grate leads into the deeper vent network.

transition.text: You squeeze through into the lower vents.

tiles.interactText: A corner booth with deep shadows. Someone has carved 'KAEL WAS HERE — AGAIN' into the table.
A stash of stolen goods behind a false panel.
A locked maintenance terminal. The login is scratched onto the back in shaky handwriting. Station security at its finest.
A locked box under the bar. Torsh hid it badly.
A pressure door leads to the outer hull derelicts. Cold, dark, and almost certainly inhabited.

transition.text: The pressure door seals behind you. The derelict district opens up.

actors.name: Dock Warden Hess
actors.bio: Twenty years at the dock gate. Hess has seen everything come through Port Sable — including, she'll quietly mention, someone who looked exactly like you. About three years ago.
ambientDialogue: Hess tracks every ship in and out of Port Sable without writing a single thing down.
"You want berth access, you talk to Rudo. You want to know what's really going on, you talk to me."

actors.name: Scav Lena
actors.bio: A young scav who works the berth row looking for jobs. She has a debt with the wrong people and is running out of time.
ambientDialogue: Lena watches the checkpoint like she's timing it.

actors.name: Impound Clerk Rudo
actors.bio: Rudo processes impound paperwork and loses at cards. His gambling debt to Fence Davi is the reason the impound key is currently negotiable.
ambientDialogue: Rudo checks his ledger, then checks the door, then checks his ledger again.
"Look, I just process the forms. What happens to the ships after that is above my clearance level."

actors.name: Trader Moss
actors.bio: Moss has run the same market stall for eleven years. She has opinions about everyone on the station and shares them freely.
ambientDialogue: Moss arranges her stock with the energy of someone who has done this ten thousand times.
"Heard someone woke up in the lower decks without knowing where they were. Probably fine."

actors.name: Fence Davi
actors.bio: Davi moves things. Where they came from and where they're going is a professional secret. Rudo owes him significantly.
ambientDialogue: Davi is always counting something, even when his hands are empty.

actors.name: Naia Voss
actors.bio: Station fixer with a perfect memory for debt and insult. Naia knows about the Wake — more than she'll say upfront. She knew the original you.
ambientDialogue: Naia watches the dock like she is pricing every mistake in sight.
"You've got his face. His walk. Even his bad habit of staring at things too long. But you're not him. Are you."

actors.name: Patch-Lan
actors.bio: A freelance medic with a sideline in things that shouldn't be possible. She is the first person to confirm what you are — and she looks unsurprised.
ambientDialogue: Patch-Lan keeps triage supplies sorted by likelihood of screaming.
"If you are not bleeding yet, good. Keep it that way."
"Clone revival is theoretical. Officially. I have done it once before. The results were... complicated."

actors.name: Commander Vask
actors.bio: Port Sable's civic authority commander. Competent, corrupt in the way everyone here is corrupt, and deeply uncomfortable about the impound file on the Scavenger's Wake.
ambientDialogue: Vask reviews impound files with the expression of someone hoping a problem will solve itself.
"That ship stays impounded. End of discussion. Do not make me say it again."

actors.name: Clerk Mori
actors.bio: Launch scheduling clerk who knows every berth window in the system and is terrified of everyone. Has a problem of their own that a resourceful person could help with.
ambientDialogue: Mori updates the launch schedule and pretends not to hear anything said around them.
"Berth E-7 has been flagged for six years. Nobody is supposed to touch that file."

actors.name: Barkeep Torsh
actors.bio: Runs the Sump Bar and has done so since before the Reavers carved out the district. Information costs a drink. The good information costs two.
ambientDialogue: Torsh polishes the bar with a rag that makes it worse.
"I heard a clone woke up somewhere in the lower levels. I heard it had a familiar face. I heard it was asking questions. I stopped listening after that."

actors.name: Kael Drin
actors.bio: A disgraced racing pilot with a valid civic transit credential he hasn't found a use for since they banned him from the circuit. He recognises the Wake on sight and goes very quiet.
ambientDialogue: Kael nurses a drink and stares at nothing in particular.
"The Wake. I know that ship. I watched it win a race it had no right to win and then disappear for six years. Where did you find it?"
"My credentials are still valid. Technically. I just stopped having a reason to use them."

actors.name: Elder Wreck-Hana
actors.bio: The oldest person in the derelict district, maybe on the station. She collects the histories of dead ships and their crews. She has heard of clone technology. She has opinions about the ethics of it that she will share whether you want them or not.
ambientDialogue: Hana sits in the same spot she has sat in for years, watching ships that will never leave.
"A backup of a person is not the same as a person. Most people believe that. I am not most people."

actors.name: Dax the Scav
actors.bio: Just trying to survive the station shift.
ambientDialogue: Dax digs through salvage with one eye on the catwalk.

actors.name: Shady Sam
actors.bio: This guy is shady, but has good luck finding things that "fell off a truck".
ambientDialogue: My wife is gonna be pissed!

tiles.interactText: Creepy Tunnel Entrance
Cell A — empty. The bunk has a note scratched into the wall: 'I know who owns the Wake. Ask Hana.'
Cell B — Talon's cell. The door is locked but the lock looks tired.
Cell C — a civic holding pen. Smells like poor decisions.
The cell lock. Civic standard. A good tech roll or the right key opens it.
Guard Sera's desk. A duty roster, a personal letter she hasn't answered, and a key ring.
An unlocked drawer with a spare key and Sera's private notes.
A crate of items taken from arrested spacers.
The cell block exit back to the Civic Quarter.

transition.text: You emerge from the jail block.

actors.name: Guard Sera
actors.bio: A civic guard who has a secret she is trying very hard not to act on. The right conversation opens her up.
ambientDialogue: Sera reads the same page of her letter for the third time.

actors.name: Talon Rhyse
actors.bio: Former convoy bruiser with a heroic streak he denies whenever possible. Currently in jail for reasons he calls 'a misunderstanding involving a Reaver and a cargo manifest'.
ambientDialogue: Talon sits on the cell bunk with the patience of someone who has been here before.
"Get me out of here and I'll owe you one. I pay my debts."

tiles.interactText: A debtor locker with civic burn marks and a half-melted tag.
A maintenance cache crammed with emergency stores.
The climb back to the drydock is unpleasant but possible.

transition.text: You climb back into the drydock.

actors.name: Orin Pike
actors.bio: A vent runner with survival instincts and flexible loyalties.

tiles.interactText: A cracked hull compartment. Old crew effects inside.
Hull markings: 'SS MERIDIAN — RACE CIRCUIT REG 4471'. A racing vessel. Old.
Sealed emergency compartment, cracked open.
Elder Hana's personal stash, hidden in plain sight.
A hull panel covered in race circuit results. The Scavenger's Wake appears in seven of them. Three wins, two disqualifications, two marked 'INCIDENT — CLASSIFIED'.
A fire pit surrounded by salvage seats. This is Dax and Hana's camp.
The communal supplies.
The pressure door back to the Sump Bar.

transition.text: You push back through the pressure door into the bar.

actors.name: Elder Wreck-Hana
actors.bio: The oldest person in the derelict district. She keeps histories of dead ships.
ambientDialogue: Hana sits watching ships that will never leave.

actors.name: Dax the Scav
actors.bio: Just trying to survive the station shift.
ambientDialogue: Dax pokes at salvage with a length of conduit.

tiles.interactText: Engine diagnostic screen. The Wake's reactor is at 67% efficiency. 'Good enough to fly, not good enough to race,' someone has written on a label.
Medbay diagnostic bed. The scanner runs a passive sweep. Something in the results would interest Patch-Lan.
The Wake's Crew Table
A scratched plaque reads: 'SCAVENGER'S WAKE — REG CIRCUIT 4471 — BUILT FOR SPEED, MODIFIED FOR EVERYTHING ELSE'
Cargo Drone L3M's charging station. The drone looks up and makes a noise that could be greeting or complaint.
Vexa's main workbench. Tools everywhere, labelled in a system only she understands. A half-repaired component sits in the vice.
The Wake's crew medical files. One file has no name — just a case number and the notation: 'CLONE SUBJECT — CYCLE 1843 — STATUS: DORMANT'.
The airlock controls. Emergency seals, pressure gauges. Everything is older than you'd like.
Ship cargo stores.
Vexa's spare parts. She'll notice if anything goes missing.
A personal note taped to the wall: 'If you are reading this and you don't know who you are yet — check the nav table. Check the hold. Don't trust the first person who tells you what you are.'
A hatch leads to the smuggler hold below.

transition.text: You drop into the hidden hold.

tiles.interactText: A cargo manifest from six years ago, still clipped to the wall. The destination is smudged. The cargo description reads 'PERSONAL EFFECTS — DO NOT INVENTORY'.
The airlock. Port Sable dock is on the other side.

transition.text: The airlock opens onto the impound bay.

tiles.interactText: Captain's Desk
Captain's Desk
Bunks. Enough for five crew. One bunk has personal items still on it — photos, a lucky token, a folded note. All from someone who expected to come back.

Navigation table. Sector charts, jump coordinates, and a locked personal log that needs a 4-digit code. The last entry before the lock was: 'If the backup wakes up, it will be confused. That is by design.'
Pilot's chair. Someone has worn a groove in the armrest. Racing habit — drumming fingers on the countdown.
Galley nook. The supplies are depleted but the equipment works. Cooking here would help crew morale.
A combination-locked storage compartment behind the pilot's chair.
Ship rations from before the impound. Still sealed.
Comms terminal. No active connections, but a stored message list shows fourteen outgoing messages to a contact listed only as 'V'. None received a reply.
Captain's Bed
Captain's Bed
Captain's Bed
Captain's Bed

actors.name: Cargo Drone L3M
actors.bio: A battered shipboard cargo drone that sounds tired despite lacking lungs.
ambientDialogue: Cargo Drone L3M clicks irritably at unsecured freight.

tiles.interactText: A cracked supply locker half buried in ash.
A half-buried supply pod from a cracked convoy.

actors.name: Boss Krail
actors.bio: A dust-ridge tyrant pretending to be a king.
ambientDialogue: Boss Krail spits black dust and sizes you up like salvage.

actors.name: Sera Flint
actors.bio: A local scout who knows the ridge paths better than the raiders do.

tiles.interactText: A relic shrine still hums beneath the waterline.
A sealed archive hatch stands ajar beneath leaking conduit light.

transition.text: You push into the archive annex.

tiles.interactText: A waterlogged transit niche full of calcified offerings.

actors.name: Reef Watcher
actors.bio: One of the few locals still willing to speak to offworlders.
ambientDialogue: The Reef Watcher keeps glancing toward the flooded archive door.

tiles.interactText: A false-floor stash hidden under magnetic struts.
A sealed locker tagged with obsolete reactor warnings.
A ladder returns to the main cargo deck.

transition.text: You climb back to the cargo deck.

tiles.interactText: A reliquary of preserved transit relics.
A trunk of salvage notes and transit tags.
The hatch opens back toward the flooded basin.

transition.text: You return to the basin walkway.

actors.name: Archivist Pell
actors.bio: Keeper of the annex records and reluctant broker of old transit truths.

tiles.interactText: Hole to surface
Odd Pile of Sand
Treasure
More Treasure
